message:
Add comprehensive compact() tests for GraphemePool

Add 359 lines of unit tests covering all compact() scenarios:

Basic operations:
- test_compact_empty_pool: Compacting empty pool is a no-op
- test_compact_no_free_slots: Compacting without gaps preserves all data
- test_compact_single_gap: Verifies single gap closure and ID remapping

Multiple gaps:
- test_compact_multiple_gaps: Non-contiguous free slots are removed
- test_compact_all_free_except_last: Only trailing slot survives
- test_compact_alternating_pattern: Free/used/free/used pattern

Edge cases:
- test_compact_with_shared_refs: Entries with refcount > 1 are preserved
- test_compact_with_duplicates: Deduped entries maintain sharing
- test_compact_idempotent: Running compact twice produces same result
- test_compact_preserves_content: All grapheme strings remain accessible

CompactionResult verification:
- Correct old_to_new mapping for moved entries
- slots_freed count matches actual reduction
- bytes_saved includes both slot and string memory
- has_remappings() returns correct boolean
- remap() helper returns None for unmoved entries

Post-compaction invariants:
- total_slots == active_count (no free slots remain)
- All active entries are accessible via remapped IDs
- Original IDs for unmoved entries still work
- Free list is empty after compaction

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
